title: Some reactions of the halide ions
summary: 9701 AS halide ion tests — Ag⁺ precipitates, ammonia complexes, and redox with concentrated sulfuric acid.
learningObjectives: Describe the silver nitrate test for halide ions.
Explain ammonia complex formation with AgCl and AgBr.
Predict products of halide reactions with concentrated H₂SO₄.
Write ionic equations for halide redox reactions.

simpleExplanation.title: Testing for X⁻
simpleExplanation.summary: Ag⁺ gives characteristic precipitates: AgCl white, AgBr cream, AgI yellow. AgCl dissolves in dilute NH₃; AgI does not.
steps: I⁻ with conc. H₂SO₄ → H₂S, S, or I₂ depending on conditions.
Halide reducing power increases I⁻ > Br⁻ > Cl⁻.

sections.content: **Halide tests** (11.3) appear in **qualitative analysis** questions. State **precipitate colour** and **NH₃ behaviour** clearly — examiners mark each observation.
